channel.title: Morning Defense
channel.description: From the SitRoom to the E-Ring, the inside scoop on defense, national security and foreign policy.

item.title: Robbing Kyiv to fight Tehran
item.description: As the Iran war approaches the four-week mark, the strain it's putting on the Ukraine war a continent away is becoming increasingly evident.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Joe Kent ‘huge’ in Tehran, small in DC
item.description: Iranian observers saw Joe Kent's resignation from the Trump administration last week as the first defection over the war, and as a sign of fragility in the MAGA base.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Iran standoff puts Taiwan chips at risk
item.description: Taiwan is scrambling to prevent its semiconductor sector from becoming a casualty of the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine, Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine, Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Trump touts Iran talks. Tehran says no dice.
item.description: Iran and the U.S. are sending mixed signals about what kind of diplomacy they’re doing — if at all.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Is there an offramp to Israel’s strikes in Lebanon?
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Takaichi’s crisis averted
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi might just be able to salvage her Washington visit.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Phelim Kine and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Phelim Kine and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe double down on Trump’s war
item.description: The nation’s top intelligence officials are lining up behind the Trump administration’s narrative that the U.S. had been under imminent threat from Iran before attacking Tehran.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Trump’s other war: on Anthropic
item.description: The government’s escalating legal battle with Anthropic is sowing confusion in Silicon Valley — and tech investors warn that a protracted fight is a terrible signal to send to the industry.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Dems on the war powers warpath
item.description: The war powers push Senate Democrats are leading this week is nearly bound to fail.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Ramadan’s unwanted guest: Trump’s war
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Kyiv’s time to shine
item.description: The war in Iran could be Ukraine’s big chance to prove its value as a partner to the United States.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Iran strikes expose US counterterrorism gaps
item.description: The war with Iran is increasing concerns in Washington about terror attacks from Tehran or its proxies.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow, Eric Bazail-Eimil and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow, Eric Bazail-Eimil and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Iran War threatens Board of Peace
item.description: President Donald Trump’s signature Board of Peace has made little progress towards getting up and running since the president — and a phalanx of world leaders — heralded its creation last month.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Iraq stands to lose and lose from Iran strikes
item.description: With the U.S. easing sanctions on Russian oil, and energy prices spiking while the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed, there are clear winners and losers emerging among oil producers.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Interview: What Iranian Kurds want
item.description: A possible U.S. move to arm Iranian Kurds would lend the group a historic boost while running the risk of pushing Iran into further disarray.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: The other simmering conflict on Iran’s border
item.description: A border spat between Pakistan and Afghanistan may add to the chaos unfurled by the U.S- Israeli joint assault on Iran.
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Why Gulf countries aren’t hitting back at Iran
item.description: For days, Gulf countries enduring Iranian strikes have stressed that they may respond in kind, but five days into punishing assaults they have held off on joining the fray.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Why Trump won’t find a Delcy in Tehran
item.description: President Donald Trump seems to be looking for a Delcy Rodriguez to emerge in Tehran. He’ll be hard pressed to find one.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Trump admin tiptoes around the word ‘war’
item.description: What is the Trump administration calling U.S. operations against Iran? It changes from moment to moment.
item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Phelim Kine
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Phelim Kine

item.title: NATO’s Ukraine lifeline hits a production wall
item.dc:creator: By Felicia Schwartz, Paul McLeary and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Felicia Schwartz, Paul McLeary and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Keep calm and World Cup on
item.description: With competing teams — not to mention millions of fans — expected to descend on Mexico in just over 100 days for the World Cup, Mexican and FIFA officials are hammering the message: Everything is under control.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Eric Bazail-Eimil
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Eric Bazail-Eimil

item.title: Trump weighs in on FISA
item.description: President Donald Trump has finally decided where he stands on a controversial foreign spy statute — and he is starting to make it known to Congress.
item.dc:creator: By John Sakellariadis and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By John Sakellariadis and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Iran looms over the State of the Union speech
item.description: More than any other foreign policy or national security issue, Washington is waiting to see if President Donald Trump will announce strikes against Iran in tonight’s State of the Union address.
item.dc:creator: By Eric Bazail-Eimil and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Eric Bazail-Eimil and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: The Diego Garcia showdown
item.description: The status of Diego Garcia — an island in the Indian Ocean’s Chagos Archipelago that is home to a joint U.S.-U.K. military base — is roiling Washington-London ties as the Trump administration mulls possible military strikes on Iran.
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: To strike or not to strike
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Coalition of the shilling: Trump’s Board of Peace
item.description: Some two dozen member countries joined the first meeting of the Board of Peace in Washington today with more than $16 billion in financial pledges.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow, Eli Stokols and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow, Eli Stokols and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Indonesia’s president has an offer for Trump
item.description: Indonesia’s President Prabowo Subianto is in Washington this week to show he’s all-in for President Donald Trump’s Board of Peace.
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Barrack’s close Epstein ties draw little fire
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to Turkey and special envoy to Syria TOM BARRACK had a yearslong friendship with convicted sex offender JEFFREY EPSTEIN, but their ties have not generated the same firestorm as others in Trump’s orbit.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: At Munich, a world order that ‘no longer exists’
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Venezuela state of play: Oil over elections
item.description: Five weeks post-Operation Absolute Resolve and Venezuela is no closer to democratic elections.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Eric Bazail-Eimil and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Eric Bazail-Eimil and Daniella Cheslow
